segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a black screen, then transitions to a dynamic montage of a woman performing various gym exercises, including dumbbell presses, pull-ups, and lunges. This is set against an upbeat electronic music track featuring a rhythmic beat and male vocals singing "Check up my body, join the flow." Bold white text overlays the footage, reading 'WEEK OF WORKOUTS' and '4-DAY SPLIT FOR BEGINNERS', as the woman works out in a modern, well-lit gym with visible equipment and other gym-goers. The scene then cuts to a woman, now in a bright, minimalist bedroom, wearing a loose gray sweatshirt and a delicate necklace. The upbeat music fades as she speaks directly to the camera with an enthusiastic and clear voice, greeting viewers with "What's up, you guys? Welcome back to another video. Another week of workouts." She wishes a "Happy New Year," noting this is her first video of 2024, and expresses happiness about the positive feedback from a similar previous video. As text 'HELLO 2024' appears on screen, she explains the content will be "very helpful to anyone who's trying to jump start their fitness journey in 2024," especially those new or less confident in the gym. She details "four beginner-friendly workouts" filmed at the gym where she was "miked up" to talk viewers through each exercise. She then emphasizes the "four-day split," with on-screen text listing 'PUSH DAY', 'LEG DAY', and 'PULL DAY', as she verbally outlines "one push day, one leg day, one pull day." The natural, soft lighting of the room highlights the neatly made bed, framed artwork, and ceiling fan.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with Kris Hui, a woman with long dark hair, wearing a loose gray sweatshirt and a delicate necklace, standing in a bright, minimalist bedroom. She speaks directly to the camera with expressive hand gestures, her voice clear, articulate, and friendly, explaining her workout split. As she discusses a "full body day," and mentions that "everyone's split may be different," large white text overlays sequentially appear on the left side of the screen, displaying 'PUSH DAY', 'LEG DAY', 'PULL DAY', and 'FULL BODY'. She emphasizes hitting "a little bit of everything" and the flexibility of adding a full body day. Midway through, a split-screen montage appears, visually illustrating her points with scenes of a woman in athletic wear on a beach labeled 'FITNESS', a person running on a grassy path, and a woman performing a squat with dumbbells in a gym labeled 'LIFESTYLE'. Kris Hui continues her dialogue with animated gestures, discussing the importance of enjoying "other activities" like "getting outside, playing a recreational sport, taking a workout class," highlighting how her split allows for rest and other pursuits. She expresses pride in having "fully filmed this, mic'd up," acknowledging the video is "a little bit lengthier than usual" but reassuring viewers that everything is "split up into chapters." Towards the end, the screen transitions to a YouTube playlist interface titled 'WEEK OF WORKOUTS' by 'Kris Hui', displaying thumbnails of various workout videos. She instructs viewers on how to "refer back to this video," "skim through that," and "check the description" for "all the workouts typed out," encouraging them to "screenshot it and take it with you to the gym," before promoting her other "week of workouts" content.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a woman, identified as 'The Woman,' standing in a bright, minimalist bedroom, wearing a loose gray sweatshirt and a delicate necklace. She speaks directly to the camera with expressive hand gestures, mentioning an entire playlist of exercises, encouraging viewers to check it out, and inviting them to follow her on Instagram at @sanfranfrisco. She promises to see them very soon in the gym for their first workout of the week. The scene then transitions to the woman in a modern gym environment, now in a light gray sports bra and olive green athletic shorts. She performs 'testing' sounds to check her audio before welcoming viewers, stating this is their 'first workout of the week' and they are doing a 'push day' focusing on 'chest, shoulders, and triceps.' On-screen text appears, confirming 'WORKOUT #1' and 'PUSH DAY — CHEST, SHOULDERS, TRICEPS'. She explains the importance of warming up for at least five minutes, whether for a full-body workout or specific muscle groups. She demonstrates front/lateral raises with dumbbells, completing 10 reps as a warm-up, describing it as a 'front raise, lateral raise combo.' Her reflection is visible in a large mirror as she speaks. She then mentions using a resistance band, which she will grab, and states she'll focus more on the workout itself rather than diving too deep into the warm-ups. As she performs banded mobility exercises for 5 minutes, using a resistance band to stretch and mobilize her shoulders, she notes that these 'up and overs' feel 'really good.' Throughout the gym segment, background sounds of clanking weights are audible, emphasizing the structured fitness environment with visible equipment like weight racks and machines.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with the Woman in Gym, a woman with long dark hair tied back, wearing a light gray sports bra and olive green athletic shorts, standing in a modern, well-lit gym space. She holds a pair of dumbbells and addresses the camera, explaining that today's workout will mostly be done on a bench but will start with an exercise using "light to medium dumbbells." As on-screen text 'DB CROSSBODY RAISES 4 X 10 REPS PER SIDE' appears, she begins performing dumbbell crossbody raises, lifting one dumbbell diagonally across her body with an underhand or supinated grip, alternating sides with controlled movements. She describes the motion as "crossing over with control" and notes it targets the "interior delts as well as the chest." Her reflection is visible in a large mirror behind her, showcasing her form from multiple angles as she emphasizes alternating sides and using this as a warm-up to transition into "heavier shoulder and chest moves." She instructs to perform "10 per side," counting to 20, and advises keeping the "core tight" without "swinging the weight up," ensuring the "specific muscle group" does all the work. The camera occasionally shifts to side and rear views, capturing her technique. After completing the set, she transitions to a bench press setup, sitting on a weight bench with dumbbells, and the on-screen text changes to 'DB BENCH PRESS 4 X 8-10 REPS'. She explains she's "grabbing a bench that is adjustable" so she can use it as a flat or incline bench for the next exercise. The gym environment, with its weight racks, dumbbell stands, and other equipment under bright lighting, along with other gym-goers, provides an active backdrop to her clear, instructional dialogue.

segments.integrated_caption: The video segment opens with the Woman in Gym, wearing a light gray sports bra and olive green athletic shorts, seated on a weight bench. She addresses the camera in a clear, instructional voice, explaining that they will be doing a "dumbbell chest press" and advises viewers to "grab a weight that you can manage," encouraging them to increase weight in later sets. As on-screen text displays 'DB BENCH PRESS 4 X 8-10 REPS', she prepares to "launch myself back here," with a slight grunt, settling onto the bench. She further explains her form, positioning her dumbbells in a "slight diagonal" to protect her shoulders. She then performs dumbbell bench presses, lowering and pressing the weights in a controlled motion, accompanied by sounds of exertion and breathing, stating she's "going for 10 reps." The modern, well-lit gym environment, with mirrored walls reflecting her movements and various Gym Equipment in the background, provides a clean aesthetic. After completing her set, she sits up, places the dumbbells on the floor, and announces, "we are going to be adjusting this bench now." Mechanical sounds of adjustment accompany her as she moves the bench’s backrest "straight up," explaining that her bench "automatically moves the seat" but others might need manual adjustment. She then picks up a new pair of lighter dumbbells, and as the on-screen text changes to 'DB SHOULDER PRESS 4 X 8-10 REPS', she introduces the next exercise: "a dumbbell shoulder press." She explains her routine, stating that "on all my push days, I like to do a chest press variation and then a shoulder press variation," considering them "staples." She sits upright, holding the dumbbells at shoulder height, preparing to begin the shoulder press, with her small microphone clipped to her bra visible throughout.

segments.integrated_caption: In a modern, well-lit gym, a woman in a light gray sports bra and olive green shorts is seen seated on a weight bench, performing dumbbell shoulder presses, with 'DB SHOULDER PRESS 4 X 8-10 REPS' displayed on-screen. She begins by explaining her routine, stating, "I will start my push days with," and demonstrates how to "kick them up like so" if weights are heavy, accompanied by a grunt and the distinct clank of dumbbells. As she lifts the weights from shoulder height to above her head, maintaining controlled motion, she instructs, "From here, we press," with audible exertion and heavy breathing. She provides detailed form advice, noting, "you want the dumbbells to be at this slight angle, not directly like this. Just to protect our shoulders again." The camera shifts, highlighting her muscular definition and focus through front, side, and rear views, as she continues her reps, punctuated by more grunts, heavy breathing, and the distinct clanking of the dumbbells. She further clarifies the proper range of motion, advising to "stop right around here. And push up. You don't want to go too far down... You want to go right around 90 degrees and push up." Mid-set, she candidly remarks, "Ooh, this is getting heavy," and later, "I'm gonna really have to kick these up," before correcting a potential error, "Also, that does not count as a rep. We start from zero here." In the background, other gym-goers use various equipment, and her reflection is visible in the mirrored walls, as she maintains concentration, concluding a repetition with a final "Ooh" of effort.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of a woman in a light gray sports bra and olive green athletic shorts, her long dark hair tied back in a ponytail, as she adjusts her grip on a pair of black dumbbells in a modern, well-lit gym. She turns to face the camera, speaking in a clear, instructional voice, introducing the "first superset" of "two exercises back-to-back." She explains she'll be doing "lateral raises" with dumbbells, noting she's been in the gym for "seven years maybe" and "can't go very heavy on these." On-screen text appears, reinforcing 'LATERAL RAISES 4 X 10 REPS' and 'DB SKULL CRUSHERS 4 X 10 REPS'. She then turns her back to the camera, performing standing lateral raises with controlled movements, describing the technique of "raising both weights" from the sides of the body with "a slight bend in the elbow." She advises against going "too heavy" to maintain isolated movement. Transitioning to a seated position on a weight bench, she continues the lateral raises, suggesting this helps "eliminate your upper body from helping." After an "Oh my gosh" exclamation, accompanied by the sound of weights clanking, she adjusts the bench to a flat position. She then introduces the next exercise, "dumbbell skull crushers," which she calls "one of my favorite tricep exercises," as she lies back on the bench to perform them with proper technique, her arms extended overhead. The bright gym environment, featuring mirrored walls, an 'EQUINOX' weight rack, and other gym-goers using treadmills and machines, remains visible throughout, emphasizing the structured fitness setting.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens in a modern, well-lit gym, featuring mirrored walls and 'EQUINOX' branded dumbbells. A woman, dressed in a light gray sports bra and olive green athletic shorts, lies supine on a flat weight bench, performing dumbbell skull crushers. On-screen text displays 'LATERAL RAISES 4 X 10 REPS' and 'DB SKULL CRUSHERS 4 X 10 REPS'. The woman's clear, instructional voice guides the viewer, explaining the exercise form: "we're gonna keep our biceps exactly where we are, straight in a line. The only thing moving is our forearms." She advises on weight selection, noting, "I could probably go a little heavier, but ooh, I'm feeling the burn," followed by audible exertion, heavy breathing, and a strained "Oh my god," highlighting the exercise's intensity. After completing the set, she sits up, checks her phone, and her voice states, "We repeat that super set four times." She then transitions to a gray wall, performing a wall sit while explaining the next exercise: "The next we're going to be doing a seated hammer front raise... I wanted to spice it up a little and make this a wall sit instead." As she demonstrates the 'WALL SIT HAMMER FRONT RAISE 3 X 10 REPS PER SIDE', she continues to instruct, detailing her posture: "My legs are pretty much 90 degrees. From here, I'm gonna be alternating, doing a hammer front raise. Hammer just meaning my palm will stay..." The camera captures her from multiple angles, emphasizing her proper form against the backdrop of various gym machines.

segments.integrated_caption: The video segment opens with the Woman in Gym, a woman with long dark hair wearing a light gray sports bra and olive green athletic shorts, performing a wall sit hammer front raise against a gray wall in a modern gym. She raises one dumbbell at a time in a controlled motion, maintaining a stable wall sit position, stating aloud, "I'm doing 10 per side, so counting to 20 in my head." segments.integrated_caption: The video opens in a well-lit, modern gym as the Woman in Gym, dressed in a blue sports bra and black athletic shorts, stands facing the camera. On-screen text 'DUMBBELL ROLLS 4 X 8-10 REPS' provides context. She immediately begins speaking, discussing the 'angle at which you hold the weight,' noting 'it's just personal preference, but I do like to switch it up some days.' After a brief 'Oh sorry, you're good, you're good' to someone off-camera, she bends to 'carefully lift the weights up,' picking up a pair of black dumbbells from the floor. As she stands and begins the exercise, hinging at the hips, she instructs, 'My shoulders in place, keeping my chest proud. I'm going to hinge at the hips.' She demonstrates the dumbbell roll with a 'slight bend in the knees' as she comes down, explaining she's only coming 'to about this far and driving back up.' She emphasizes keeping 'your neck in line with your spine,' advising to keep 'your eyes on the floor, about three feet in front of you,' to avoid straining the neck. She offers vivid cues like 'pretend like you are closing a door behind you, while all time simultaneously picking up something off the ground,' and clarifies that 'your shins here are staying in place, the only thing moving back is your butt, your quads.' She suggests practicing 'without a weight first' if desired. Throughout her detailed instructions, the ambient sounds of a busy gym, including the hum of equipment and distant chatter, are audible. She maintains precise form, occasionally gesturing, before placing the dumbbells back on the floor and kneeling slightly, continuing her explanation as the segment ends.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a woman, wearing a blue sports bra and black athletic shorts, standing on a black plyo box labeled '24"' and '6"', preparing for deficit reverse lunges. On-screen text in the upper left corner reads 'DEFICIT REVERSE LUNGES 4 X 8 REPS PER LEG'. She explains her setup, stating, "I'm going to use to elevate my front foot, and this is just going to give me a little bit more leverage," contrasting it with doing the exercise on the ground where her "knee would bonk." She adds, "This gives me like this much extra room to work with." She then discusses her equipment choice, noting, "You could use dumbbells, I like kettlebells just because of the grip," and clarifies, "whether you want to hold two kettlebells or one is up to you, but I'm just going to do one." She begins the exercise, explaining, "working on my left leg first, I'm going to step back with my right." She steps off the box with one leg, lowering her body into a lunge, then picks up a black kettlebell, holding it in one hand, and continues performing the lunges with controlled movements. Her posture remains upright, and she uses her arms for balance. She describes her technique: "I'm stepping back and driving through that front leg. I'm also coming down with as much control as possible. I'm just doing eight per side." She emphasizes the importance of control, stating, "If you are in control of this movement, you're not going to be banging your knee on the ground. Barely tapping it at most." The gym environment features black rubber flooring, mirrored walls, and various equipment, all brightly lit. Throughout the segment, upbeat, rhythmic background music plays, complementing her focused demonstration.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens in a well-lit, modern gym with mirrored walls, where a woman in a red sports bra and gray leggings begins her workout. She lies on her side, performing foam rolling on a black foam roller with orange end caps, targeting her upper back and glutes. Her voice explains, "we have a pull day, so back and biceps. I'm just foam rolling first because I'm pretty freaking sore from a leg day." She then transitions to a prone position to continue rolling her upper back, noting that "Foam rolling my back also feels really nice." She elaborates on her foam rolling routine, mentioning she usually starts with her legs and has learned from YouTube follow-along videos. She adds that she doesn't do it before every workout but tries to a few times a week, especially as she's "currently training for a marathon" and needs it more for recovery in 2024. segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with the Woman in Gym, dressed in a red sports bra and gray leggings, performing bent-over rows with a barbell using a supinated grip, as the on-screen text 'BENT OVER ROW (SUPINATED GRIP) 4 X 10 REPS' confirms. She explains her technique, stating, "from here I'm just going to hinge and stay in this position. And I'm just going to row," while demonstrating controlled form, bending at the hips with a straight back. As she pulls the barbell towards her lower chest, grunts of exertion are heard, and she adds, "Pulling the bar to my stomach, controlling the weight on the way down." She continues the set, counting "Nine, ten" amidst further grunts. After completing the set, she transitions to a crouched position against a gray wall, speaking directly to the camera with expressive hand gestures. She elaborates on the workout's progression, mentioning, "On our back workout so far we've done like an overhand grip with the lap pull down. We've done more of a neutral grip with that single arm row, also neutral grip with the seated row, and now we are doing underhand. So we're we're hitting it all." She explains that "Switching up the grip changes the exercise a little bit, just targets different areas." Returning to the barbell, she adjusts her grip and resumes the bent-over rows, maintaining consistent form. The bright, modern gym environment, with its black rubber flooring, mirrored walls, and white dumbbell rack, is visible, and a camera angle shift reveals a TV screen and a Man in White Tank Top performing pull-ups in the background through the mirror. She continues to offer form advice, stating, "for this you also want to keep a neutral neck and spine," and humorously adds, "Ah, talking and rowing is hard." She further instructs, "Looking in front of you rather than looking down, or looking up. Neutral neck, y'all," as sounds of her exertion accompany her final movements.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a woman in a gray sports bra and leggings, crouched against a gray wall, reviewing workout data on a small black device and her phone. On-screen text displays 'CIRCUIT #1' with a list of four exercises and '4 ROUNDS'. She speaks, explaining that "this first circuit is going to be four rounds of four exercises performed back to back to back to back," emphasizing that "the magic number today is eight. So eight reps." She then transitions to kneeling on a black mat, placing down a pair of black dumbbells labeled '20 IRON GRIP', while remarking on her preference for simple workouts that can be done at home with just a mat and a couple sets of weights. She details her equipment, mentioning a "heavier set of dumbbells" for squat, shoulder press, and row, and a "lighter pair" for arm curls and tricep extensions. As she stands, she announces, "I'm going to be starting with one of my heavier dumbbells and the first exercise is going to be a single arm squat thruster." She begins performing squat thrusters, holding one dumbbell at a time, alternating sides while extending the other arm out for balance. The on-screen text updates to 'SQUAT THRUSTERS (8 PER SIDE)' as she demonstrates the movement, explaining, "starting with the weight loaded at my shoulder. I'm going to squat and thrust up. All in one motion." Her hair flows with each powerful overhead press, and her focused, controlled motions are accompanied by audible exertion sounds and heavy breathing. She confirms the rep count, stating "eight per side," and further explains her technique, saying, "I'm letting the momentum of that squat help press up," before concluding the set, mentioning "after eight." The gym environment features a neutral gray wall, dark rubber flooring, and a stationary bike in the background, illuminated by natural light from a window.

She instructs, "Feet are down, hips are up. From here, I'm gonna go left chest press, right chest press, together. That's one. We do eight," while lifting the weights overhead and keeping her hips elevated. She continues to breathe heavily, offering advice, "If you need to rest, just hold it here at the top and start right back up. Tuck your belly into your spine to keep those hips up." After completing the set, she sits up, smiles, and gestures, exclaiming "Wow!" and "Full body," appearing energized. She then stands, holding the dumbbells at her sides, ready for the next movement, as the text changes to 'RDL TO BENT OVER ROW (8)'. segments.integrated_caption: The video opens in a modern, well-lit gym with dark rubber flooring and recessed lighting, where a Woman in Gym, wearing a gray sports bra and leggings, stands upright, holding a pair of black dumbbells labeled '20 IRON GRIP'. She immediately begins speaking, instructing, "Now, we're gonna rise up. We're gonna do an RDL to bent over row," explaining that they've done RDLs before and are now adding the row. The on-screen text confirms 'CIRCUIT #1 RDL TO BENT OVER ROW (8)'. She performs Romanian Deadlifts (RDLs) by hinging at the hips, then transitions into bent-over rows, pulling the dumbbells toward her torso, while coaching, "RDL, stop here, row. Nice, slow, controlled. What's the rush? We bend, row, leading with our elbows." During these movements, her focused expression is visible, and sounds of her exertion (breathing, grunts) are heard, accompanied by the clanking of the dumbbells. The camera shifts to a side view, revealing a row of treadmills and a black stationary bike in the background. After completing the set, she places the dumbbells on the floor, stating, "Okay, let's just let's forget about those weights for this last exercise. I'm gonna need room all around. So, to the side and then also behind me." The on-screen text updates to 'CIRCUIT #1 LATERAL LUNGE TO 3 PLANK JACKS (8)'. She then performs lateral lunges, stepping wide to the side, bending the knee, and returns to center, instructing, "I'm going lateral lunge left, lateral lunge right." She drops into a plank position, performing three plank jacks while counting, "Drop into a plank, three plank jacks. Two, three," before returning to standing, saying "Stand it up," and continuing to count "One, two, oh" with a slight grunt. Her movements are controlled and deliberate, showcasing her form and focus, all set against a backdrop of upbeat background gym music and general gym ambiance.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a Woman in Gym, wearing a gray sports bra and leggings, white socks, and sneakers, performing a fluid dynamic movement sequence from a bent-over position into standing, then a lunge, and finally a plank-like position, showcasing core engagement. This is set against a modern gym backdrop with a black stationary bike, dark rubber flooring, and recessed lighting, all accompanied by continuous upbeat electronic music. She has a smartwatch on her left wrist and a microphone clipped to her bra. After completing the movement, she turns to face the camera, smiling with her long dark hair tied back, and speaks with a slightly breathless tone, "Um, we are officially sweating. Yeah." She continues, explaining the workout structure, "So we just finished four rounds of four exercises. Now we have three rounds of three exercises. Quick rest." On-screen text in the upper left corner displays 'CIRCUIT #2' with 'ZOTTMAN CURLS (8)', 'PROWLER PUSH-UPS (8)', 'OVERHEAD TRICEP EXTENSION (8)', and '3 ROUNDS'. She advises, "Short rest in between the rounds, but take a longer rest in between the circuits." Holding a white water bottle and gesturing, she announces, "Phase two. We got a little bit of upper body work, so I'm gonna grab my lighter set of dumbbells." She sets the bottle down and picks up a pair of black dumbbells, which she identifies as "12.5" (despite being visually labeled '20 IRON GRIP'), and begins demonstrating Zottman curls. She explains the technique, "We're gonna start with Zottman curls. Basically a bicep curl, but once you curl up, you flip your wrists so your palms are facing down and you descend like that." She continues the demonstration, adding, "Flip it back. Two. Magic number is eight," with audible sounds of exertion and breathing, concluding with "Oh my god. Pain." The consistent natural lighting from windows illuminates the gray walls throughout her energetic and instructional delivery.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with a woman in a gray sports bra and leggings kneeling on a black exercise mat, gripping two black dumbbells labeled '20 IRON GRIP'. She immediately begins speaking in a clear, instructional tone, stating, "Okay. Right away, coming down to the floor with my weights. Next, I have eight prowler push-ups." As on-screen text in the upper left corner displays 'CIRCUIT #2' and 'PROWLER PUSH-UPS (8)', she transitions into a plank position, explaining, "And there's different variations, I'll go through them with you, but starting in this plank position." She then demonstrates the prowler push-ups, lowering her chest toward the dumbbells and pushing back up, describing the motion: "From here, we shoot our butt back, feel the stretch in the back, launch back forward, push up." She adds that it "should be a pretty nice fluid motion." She continues to perform the controlled, deliberate push-ups, making sounds of exertion, and comments, "Oh, yeah, I like that." After completing the set, she returns to a kneeling position, then stands up, picking up the dumbbells and holding them at her sides. She continues to explain variations, mentioning, "Eight of those. You could eliminate the push-up completely," and demonstrates a modified plank to sit back, describing it as "a burner as well. You could do this on your knees." Throughout, subtle background gym sounds, including muffled speech and clanking, are audible. She concludes by suggesting, "You could either do two lighter weights or one heavier," as she faces the camera, appearing to explain the next step in the brightly lit gym environment with a gray wall and dark rubber flooring.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a woman in a gray sports bra and matching leggings, a smartwatch, and a microphone clipped to her bra, performing overhead tricep extensions against a gray wall. She uses black '20 IRON GRIP' dumbbells, with on-screen text indicating 'CIRCUIT #2 OH TRICEP EXTENSION (8)'. She explains her form, stating, "now I'm going to go into eight tricep extensions. So holding the weights above head, reach down, extend up," and emphasizes keeping "My biceps... right by my ears the entire time. I'm not letting my elbows flare out too much." She continues, mentioning, "You could also do these kneeling, which I'm currently doing right now," as she completes the set with controlled movements, accompanied by sounds of exertion. After finishing, she sits, places the dumbbells aside, and checks her phone. The on-screen text updates to 'CIRCUIT #3' listing 'LAT PULLOVER TO SUITCASE CRUNCH (8)', 'STRAIGHT LEG SIT-UP (8)', and 'GLUTE BRIDGE MARCHES (8)' for '3 ROUNDS'. She then introduces the next segment, saying, "Okay. Yep. Those are your three upper body moves. Three rounds. And to finish off, we will be ending with some abs... Little ab finisher, three rounds of three exercises. Let's get it." She lies on a black exercise mat on the dark rubber flooring, performing lat pullovers with a black kettlebell, transitioning into suitcase crunches. She describes the movement: "Dumbbell lat pullover to a suitcase crunch. Extending both my arms and my legs out to flat and then I'm crunching everything in, squeezing at the top. I'm keeping my dumbbell..." as the segment ends, with her movements and explanations clearly audible.

segments.integrated_caption: The video segment features a Woman in Gym, dressed in a gray sports bra and matching leggings, white socks, and sneakers, performing Circuit #3 of a full-body workout on a black exercise mat against a gray wall. She begins with 'Lat Pullover to Suitcase Crunch (8)', lying on her back and holding a black kettlebell overhead. Her clear, instructional voice explains, "If you had a medicine ball, you could sort of transfer the weight... but we're just going to keep it simple. We're just going to hold on to this. We're going for eight. Squeeze as hard as you can at the top." She also advises, "you don't have to use a weight for this, but if you want an extra added challenge, go for it." The on-screen text updates to 'Straight Leg Sit-Up (8)', and she transitions to lying flat, stating, "Next, I'm going to stay in this position and from here, I'm just going to sit up," demonstrating a controlled sit-up while instructing to "roll into a nice straight L and slowly roll back down," repeating "Sit it up. Slow roll back down. Eight of these." Finally, the text changes to 'Glute Bridge Marches (8)'. She lifts her hips into a glute bridge, explaining, "Last but not least, we're going to get back into that glute bridge hold, but we are going to march," and counts "One, two," while emphasizing to "Try to keep your weight distributed evenly between your" legs. Throughout the exercises, an upbeat, non-lyrical electronic music track plays in the background. Her smartwatch is visible on her left wrist, and a microphone is clipped to her bra, indicating a recorded session in the clean, modern gym setting with dark rubber flooring, a black stationary bike, and '20 IRON GRIP' dumbbells nearby.
